settings.name: Tuberose Absolute
settings.story: Hand-picked at midnight in southern France when the flower opens. We use 2.4kg of petals per kg of absolute — the reason your skin smells like a garden after.

settings.name: Glycerin from Beetroot
settings.story: Plant-derived humectant pulls water into the skin while you wash. Why this body wash leaves you softer than you started, even in Singapore aircon.

settings.name: Coco Glucoside
settings.story: Sugar + coconut, full stop. Lifts dirt without stripping the skin barrier. Mild enough for daily use on every body zone we say yes to.

settings.heading: One full pump
settings.body: That's ~2.5ml. Stop there. More foam, less product.

settings.heading: Lather, then linger
settings.body: Massage into wet skin for 20 seconds. The scent develops as it warms.

settings.heading: Rinse, leave the smell
settings.body: Skin will smell for hours. That's the wash, not your fragrance — wear something complementary.

settings.q: Can I refill an old Postcard bottle from years ago?
settings.a: Yes — any age, any world. Even if the label has faded. Bring it in, we wash and refill.

settings.q: What if I want to switch worlds in the same bottle?
settings.a: Bring it in empty. We rinse with citric acid, dry, and refill with whatever world you fancy. No charge for the rinse.
Yes — S$4 for a fresh brass pump if yours stops. Drop in any time, swap on the spot.

settings.q: Is it safe for sensitive skin?
settings.a: Yes — coco glucoside is one of the gentlest cleansers in cosmetics. Patch test if you are reactive to florals (the tuberose absolute is potent).

settings.q: What about the empty refill pouch?
settings.a: Drop it in any postbox with the prepaid label, we sterilise and reuse. Or recycle the aluminium yourself.
